headline: Vice President Kamala Harris Courts Corporate Leaders Amidst Tight Biden-Trump Race: Report
summary: Kamala Harris actively courts corporate leaders amidst a competitive Biden-Trump race, seeking to bridge gaps and secure business support through strategic engagements.

headline: Jensen Huang And Nvidia: Riding High On AI, But For How Long?
summary: In a historic moment for Nvidia, CEO Jensen Huang, aged 61, witnessed his company momentarily eclipse Microsoft as the most valuable company globally, boasting a market valuation of $3.34 trillion.

headline: Tesla Analysts Temper Expectations, Fisker Shuts Down Shop, Nikola's 30-for-1 Reverse Split And More: Biggest EV Stories Of The Week
summary: Standing as a testament to the challenges faced by EV startups, another upstart has gone bankrupt, and those remaining in the industry are also finding the going tough.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Nvidia, Trump Media, GameStop, Apple And Dogecoin Faces 'Decision Time'
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors’ favorite stocks over the last week — here’s a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Wall Street Gunning For 14% Rally In Apple Stock Despite Slow 2024 So Far Compared To Mag 7 Peers: 'AI... Will Aid Multiple Years Of Growth'
summary: Apple's stock could surge by 14% as it leads in AI, with a slow year but raised price targets from CFRA and Bernstein analysts. Mag 7 stocks have outperformed except for Tesla.

headline: Apple To Exclude Key Features From iOS 18 And macOS Sequoia For EU Customers: Here's Why
summary: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) has stated that it will not be offering three major features of its upcoming operating systems to its EU customers.

headline: Jensen Huang Offloads Over $90M Worth Shares In The Last Week Since Nvidia Scaled Peak Market Capitalization Of $3.24 Trillion
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sold $94.64 million worth of shares last week amidst company's rapid surge in stock market. Concerns arise about sustainability and growth prospects.
